title: asterolasia correifolia
intro: Asterolasia correifolia, also known as the 'New South Wales white boronia', is a plant species native to the eastern coast of Australia. It is a flowering plant with fragrant white flowers that have been used for medicinal and culinary purposes. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Asterolasia correifolia can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as salves and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
